description: Streak Ring UI - Replace plain streak numbers with 7-segment ring visualizations showing last 7 days of workout activity, with explanation popups for each streak type

userStories.title: Add shared types for streak day breakdown
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared types for per-day workout data so both API and web can reference them.
acceptanceCriteria: Add StreakDayInfo interface to packages/shared/src/types.ts with fields: date (string, YYYY-MM-DD), hasWorkout (boolean), groupWorkoutId (string | null), dayLabel (string — single letter like 'M', 'T', 'W')
Add StreakDetail interface to packages/shared/src/types.ts with fields: burnStreak (number), supernovaStreak (number), last7Days (StreakDayInfo[] — length 7, index 0 = 6 days ago, index 6 = today)
Update existing GroupStats type to include last7Days: StreakDayInfo[]
Update existing ProfileStats type to include highestActiveStreakLast7Days: StreakDayInfo[] | null (the last-7-days for the relationship with the highest active streak)
Export StreakDayInfo and StreakDetail from the shared package index
Shared package builds successfully (cd packages/shared && yarn build)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extend streak calculator to return last-7-days breakdown
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the streak calculation logic to return which of the last 7 days had group workouts so the UI can render ring segments.
acceptanceCriteria: calculateStreaks() in services/api/src/services/streak-calculator.ts returns a new last7Days field: an array of 7 StreakDayInfo objects (index 0 = 6 days ago, index 6 = today)

Each StreakDayInfo contains: date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD), hasWorkout boolean, groupWorkoutId (the group workout ID if one exists that day, null otherwise), dayLabel (single-letter day-of-week e.g. 'M', 'T', 'W')
Existing burnStreak and supernovaStreak return values remain unchanged
Existing unit tests still pass (cd services/api && yarn test -- streak-calculator)
New unit tests cover last7Days for: all 7 active, none active, alternating days, streak with gaps, multiple workouts on same day (picks first groupWorkoutId)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Build shared package first (cd packages/shared && yarn build) before running API typecheck since it depends on the new StreakDayInfo type from US-001.

userStories.title: Update API streak endpoints to return last-7-days data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all streak-related API endpoints to include the last7Days array in their responses.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /burn-buddies/:id/streaks returns { burnStreak, supernovaStreak, last7Days } matching the StreakDetail type, where last7Days is StreakDayInfo[]
GET /burn-squads/:id/streaks returns { burnStreak, supernovaStreak, last7Days } matching the StreakDetail type
GET /users/:uid/profile response includes highestActiveStreakLast7Days in ProfileStats — the last7Days array from the buddy/squad that has the highest active streak
Dashboard endpoint (GET /dashboard) enriches each buddy/squad with last7Days alongside existing streak fields
Existing API tests still pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
New or updated tests verify last7Days is present and correct in burn-buddies, burn-squads, and dashboard responses
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Build shared package first (cd packages/shared && yarn build). The calculateStreaks() function already returns last7Days from US-002 — endpoints just need to pass it through in their responses.

userStories.title: Create StreakRing SVG component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my streak visualized as a ring split into 7 segments so I can quickly understand my recent workout consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: New component at apps/web/src/components/StreakRing.tsx

Accepts props: streakCount (number), last7Days (StreakDayInfo[]), color ('orange' | 'violet'), label (string), description (string), basePath (string — e.g. '/burn-buddies/abc123' used to construct group workout navigation links)
Renders an SVG ring split into 7 equal arc segments with small gaps (~4 degrees) between them
Segments start from the top (12 o'clock position), proceeding clockwise, index 0 (6 days ago) first
Each segment is filled with the variant color at full opacity if hasWorkout is true, or at ~20% opacity if false
Each segment displays the dayLabel (single letter: M, T, W, etc.) inside the arc
Filled segments with a groupWorkoutId are clickable links that navigate to {basePath}/group-workouts/{groupWorkoutId}
Clickable segments show a pointer cursor on hover
Center of the ring displays the streak count as a large bold number
Label below the ring reads the label prop (e.g. 'Burn Streak' or 'Supernova Streak')
Orange variant uses the existing --color-primary / amber theme colors for filled segments
Violet variant uses violet/purple colors (e.g. #8b5cf6 / violet-500) for filled segments
Ring is responsive — approximately 100-120px diameter on desktop, scaling down on mobile
Component is self-contained with no external SVG dependencies
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Build shared package first (cd packages/shared && yarn build) to get StreakDayInfo type. SVG arcs use basic trigonometry (startAngle/endAngle per segment). To verify visually, create a temporary test page or use the component on an existing page with mock data.

userStories.title: Add streak explanation popups to StreakRing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to understand how each streak type works so I can maintain my streaks effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: The label text below the ring in StreakRing.tsx includes a small info icon (ⓘ or an SVG info circle icon) next to the label text to signal a popup is available
Hovering (desktop) or tapping (mobile) the label+icon shows a popup/tooltip displaying the description prop text
Burn Streak description text: 'Log at least one group workout per week to keep your burn streak alive. Miss a full week (7 days) and the streak resets to zero.'
Supernova Streak description text: 'Log a group workout every single day to build your supernova streak. Miss a day and the supernova streak resets to zero.'
Popup is styled with a dark background (e.g. bg-gray-900), light text, subtle border, and rounded corners consistent with the app dark theme
Popup is positioned above or below the label and does not overflow the viewport
Popup dismisses when the cursor moves away (desktop) or user taps elsewhere (mobile)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The StreakRing component already accepts a 'description' prop from US-004. This story adds the popup UI that displays it. Use a simple CSS hover/focus approach or a lightweight state toggle — no tooltip library needed.

userStories.title: Replace streak stat cards with rings on burn buddy detail page
userStories.description: As a user viewing a burn buddy's detail page, I want to see streak rings instead of plain number cards.
acceptanceCriteria: On apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-buddies/[id]/page.tsx, the 'Burn Streak' and 'Supernova Streak' StatCard tiles are replaced with StreakRing components
Burn Streak ring uses color='orange', Supernova Streak ring uses color='violet'
Two rings are displayed side-by-side in a flex row, centered above the remaining stats grid
Pass basePath='/burn-buddies/{id}' so clickable segments link to the correct group workout detail page
Pass description text for burn streak: 'Log at least one group workout per week to keep your burn streak alive. Miss a full week (7 days) and the streak resets to zero.'
Pass description text for supernova streak: 'Log a group workout every single day to build your supernova streak. Miss a day and the supernova streak resets to zero.'
Ring segments accurately reflect the last 7 days of group workout activity from the streaks endpoint response
Remaining 6 stat cards (Highest Streak, First Workout, Total Workouts, This Month, This Week, Burn Buddy Since) are unchanged
Page fetches last7Days from the existing /burn-buddies/:id/streaks endpoint (which now includes last7Days from US-003)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Replace streak stat cards with rings on burn squad detail page
userStories.description: As a user viewing a burn squad's detail page, I want to see the same streak ring visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: On apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-squads/[id]/page.tsx, the 'Burn Streak' and 'Supernova Streak' StatCard tiles are replaced with StreakRing components
Same visual treatment as burn buddy page: orange for burn streak, violet for supernova streak
Two rings displayed side-by-side in a flex row above remaining stats grid
Pass basePath='/burn-squads/{id}' for group workout navigation links
Pass the same description texts as the burn buddy page for both streak types
Ring segments accurately reflect squad group workout activity for last 7 days
Remaining stat cards unchanged
Page fetches last7Days from the existing /burn-squads/:id/streaks endpoint
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add streak rings to friend profile page
userStories.description: As a user viewing a friend's profile, I want to see their streak visualized as rings so I can see their recent consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: On apps/web/src/app/(main)/profile/[uid]/page.tsx, add burn streak and supernova streak StreakRing components
Rings display the highestActiveStreak value as the streak count and highestActiveStreakLast7Days as the ring segments (from the ProfileStats response)
If the friend has no active streak (highestActiveStreak is null), rings show 0 in center with all segments empty (all hasWorkout: false)
Pass the same description texts for both streak types
For basePath, use the path of the buddy/squad that has the highest active streak (if available), otherwise omit clickable navigation
Streak explanation popups work identically to buddy/squad detail pages
Rings are positioned prominently in the stats section, above other stat cards
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The profile endpoint now returns highestActiveStreakLast7Days from US-003. If basePath is not determinable (e.g. the user has multiple buddies), segments can be non-clickable — just don't pass groupWorkoutIds.
